content:
{
  "title": "Meta-Prompting: Dynamic Contextualized Prompt Generation for Robustness Against LLM Jailbreaks",
  "problem": "Current LLMs are vulnerable to adversarial and jailbreak prompts that exploit rigid or static prompt structures. There is a lack of prompting methods that dynamically adapt to conversational context and user intent to prevent model misuse at inference time.",
  "motivation": "Despite increased interest in prompt engineering and safety tuning, static prompts or fixed rule-based guardrails are frequently bypassed by sophisticated adversarial attacks. As LLMs are widely deployed in real-world applications, ensuring adaptive, context-sensitive defense mechanisms is increasingly urgent. A dynamic, on-the-fly prompting mechanism could offer stronger, real-time robustness.",
  "method": "We propose Meta-Prompting, a meta-controller module that sits between the user and the LLM, dynamically generating and updating a system prompt based on recent conversation history and a learned adversarial intent detector. The meta-controller leverages a small, fine-tuned transformer to synthesize contextually relevant constraints and guidance into each new prompt, informed by both intent scores and detected attack primitives. This mechanism provides unique, context-aware prompt instructions for every query, preventing attackers from reverse-engineering a static system prompt. The meta-controller is trained with adversarial examples and guided by reinforcement learning to optimize robustness and utility trade-offs.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate Meta-Prompting on jailbreak and adversarial prompt datasets from JailbreakBench, AdvBench, and custom red-teaming corpora. Baselines include static prompt guardrails, AdaShield, and retrieval-augmented prompting. Metrics include attack success rate, utility retention (on safe tasks), and latency overhead."
}
